I couldn't find any information on Angorkis scottiana. However, I found information on Andrographis paniculata, which is commonly known as Andrographis or Kalmegh. Here's a paragraph based on this plant:

Andrographis paniculata, commonly known as Andrographis or Kalmegh, is a plant species that belongs to the family Acanthaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Andrographis paniculata include andrographolide, andrographiside, neoandrographolide, and 14-deoxyandrographolide, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Andrographis paniculata include capsules, tablets, and teas, which are used to treat various health conditions.
